name: Liberty Global Ltd.
business: We are an international provider of broadband internet, video, fixed-line telephony and mobile communications services to residential customers and businesses in Europe.

exposures.sentence: We are an international provider of broadband internet, video, fixed-line telephony and mobile communications services to residential customers and businesses in Europe.
On February 3, 2026, we announced a new strategic partnership with Google Cloud Services to embed new artificial intelligence (AI) services throughout our operating companies and European operations.
Liberty Growth has amassed investments in approximately 70 companies and funds, with concentrated investments in industry leaders such as Formula E, Televisa Univision, Edgeconnex, AtlasEdge and ITV.
Our Smart Security services offer a layer of security for all customer connected devices and provide network security, safe browsing and fraud prevention measures.
We provide premium electric car racing content through our controlling interest in Formula E.
We also have significant investments in Televisa Univision, ITV, EdgeConneX, the AtlasEdge JV and several regional sports networks.

catalysts.event: Sale of Slovakia operations expected to close
catalysts.sentence: Closing of the transaction is subject to regulatory approval, which is expected in the first half of 2026.
In 2026, we expect to begin rolling-out DOCSIS 4 across certain parts of our footprint.

catalysts.event: Introduce new Connect Boxes and Mesh Extenders supporting WiFi 7
catalysts.sentence: During 2026, we expect to add new Connect Boxes and Mesh Extenders, supporting the WiFi 7 standard, to our DOCSIS 3.1 and XGSPON devices.

catalysts.event: Belgian Competition Authority final decision on network sharing agreement
catalysts.sentence: The market test concluded at the end of November and we anticipate a final decision from the BCA in the course of Q1 2026.

catalysts.event: Smart Security service rollout to rest of footprint
catalysts.sentence: Smart Security service is anticipated to be rolled out to the rest of our footprint during 2026.
Wyre expects to further roll-out and operate an HFC and fiber-to-the-home network within Belgium, aiming to cover 70% of its footprint with FTTH by 2030 and 78% of its footprint by 2038.
Wyre expects to further roll-out and operate an HFC and fiber-to-the-home network within Belgium, aiming to cover 70% of its footprint with FTTH by 2030 and 78% of its footprint by 2038.
